name: Sensational Tomato Quiche
recipeInstructions:
Pie crust should be partially baked. Use the same oven temperature as for cooking quiche, 350 degrees Fahrenheit, and time it so the crust will bake about 4-7 minutes and cool only about 5 minutes before filling with the mixture to continue baking.
You will need 1 egg and 3 egg yolks for this recipe.
1. Saute onions, green bell peppers, and garlic in 2 tablespoons olive oil in a heavy skillet over low heat.
2. Add chopped tomatoes (with skins removed, seeds discarded) and seasonings. Stir gently and cook over low heat for 5 minutes.
3. Reduce heat to maintain the mixture's simmer without allowing it to scorch. Remove from heat and stir in tomato paste, parsley, and black pepper.
4. In a bowl, whisk together the egg, 3 egg yolks, and tomato paste until well combined. Pour this into the skillet with the tomato mixture, then distribute evenly into the partially baked pie crust.
5. Arrange olives, grated Parmesan cheese, and anchovies on top if desired.
6. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for about 30 minutes or until set and golden brown.
